db_hits.text: Notarikon is one of three principal hermeneutic techniques of practical Kabbalah (alongside *gematria* and *temurah*), in which new words are constructed from the initial or final letters of the words in a phrase, or conversely, each letter of a word is expanded into a full word. Renaissance Christian Cabalists adopted notarikon as a tool for discovering hidden Christian meanings in Hebrew Scripture — Pico della Mirandola and Reuchlin both employed the technique to argue that Old Testament texts contained encrypted references to Christ and the Trinity. Agrippa's De Occulta Philosophia treats notarikon as an operative method for constructing divine names of power.
